text:
Market today: Mixed macro backdrop. Yen at 40-year lows adds currency stress globally, and oil's biggest quarterly drop in 6 years signals demand worry - both can spook risk appetite and hit growth/tech sentiment fast. No direct tech catalyst today, so tape will be driven by momentum and macro mood.

Your holdings: All scanner statuses are HOLD/NEW - nothing flashing red. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; on margin, a bad day in that name hits harder than it looks. SOXL (+5.71%) is a 3x levered ETF - decay is real and it moves violently; your $200 stop is wide, so size matters. CRWD (+6.14%) has a scanner setup near current price but R is only 1.08 - thin reward for the risk.

Watch: 1) Oil's continued slide - if energy weakness bleeds into broad risk-off, your leveraged chip names get hit first. 2) SOXL price action around $269 - any reversal candle and that $200 stop gets relevant fast.

Bottom line: Book is healthy but margin + leverage mean your only job today is protecting gains, not adding risk.
plays: SOXL: Tighten your mental stop closer to $230-240 given 3x leverage and decay - $200 is wide enough that a real pullback costs you most of your gain before it triggers.
SPCX: Keep position small and manage manually as noted - no stop data, new IPO, margin amplifies any gap-down hard.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist but no headline catalyst backs them; hold what's working and don't force it.
